title: oxalis semiloba
intro: Oxalis semiloba, also known as the Salem peach or wood sorrel, is a perennial plant belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. It is recognized by its yellow, white, or pink flowers and edible leaves, which have a tangy, sour taste similar to lemon juice. The leaves and roots of the Oxalis semiloba plant have been used in traditional medicine for their purported medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help treat various health issues. Oxalis semiloba can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with herbalists recommending its use to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems.
